what_it_does: Renders the node-type palette region: it reads node types from the AppContext registry (oinfo), groups them by their declared kind, and displays each type as an actionable shared-kit Surface card inside a tone-coded lane. Clicking a card calls addNode(t) to add that node type to the board.
observations: Uses shared kit components SectionHead, LaneHead, Surface, and EmptyState.
Maps kinds to design-system tones: process -> 'sig' (mint), content -> 'wire' (blue), anything else -> 'dim'.
Groups registry keys by oinfo[t]?.kind with a fallback 'other' lane.
Sorts kinds so process and content appear first, then remaining kinds alphabetically.
Each card shows a two-letter uppercase monogram, the type name, and a '＋ add' cue.
Preserves the original data-ui-ref='ui://rail/palette' and the addNode click handler.
Renders an EmptyState when the registry has no types.
Heavy inline commentary describes the redesign rationale and preserved behavior.
standouts: Recognition-by-sight rack structure: node types grouped into lanes by kind rather than a flat list.
Color/spine tone is the primary kind signal, avoiding a repeated 'kind' word per row.
Fail-soft grouping: unknown or forward-provision kinds land in a neutral 'other' lane instead of being dropped or mislabeled.
Pure markup pass: no new state or controller logic added.
conventions: Follows the shared kit component pattern (components/kit.tsx).
Uses a design-system kind vocabulary (process/content) mapped to tone tokens.
Uses explicit, narrative comments with region markers (F0, WAVE-4).
Exports a single functional React component as the file's public API.
concerns: The title attribute is built with string concatenation rather than a localized/template string approach.
The monogram is always the first two uppercase characters, which may collide or be unclear for some type names.
Unknown kinds are rendered under their raw kind name with a 'dim' tone, which could expose internal/forward-provision names to operators.
No keyboard/accessibility handling beyond the Surface component's presumed behavior; not visible in this file.
notes: A refactored palette region that turns a flat node-type registry into grouped, color-coded kit cards while keeping the original add-node interaction untouched.
questions: What is the exact shape of oinfo entries beyond .kind?
What props do LaneHead and Surface accept, and how do they apply the tone?
Are 'present' and 'model' kinds intentionally filtered out, or just never present in the current registry?
How is the data-ui-ref consumed by tests or instrumentation?
Does addNode handle the same type being added repeatedly?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a redesigned palette region that lists registry node types as grouped, actionable cards and preserves the original addNode behavior.
apparent_themes: Design-system consistency between palette and canvas node cards
Minimal behavioral change during visual refactor
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the palette scannable at a glance by organizing node types into semantic lanes and using color-coded spines/monograms to communicate node kind without forcing the operator to read metadata on every row.
novelty: Distinctive grouped-lane layout with kind-tone monograms and an explicit '＋ add' cue, turning a registry dump into a visual vocabulary aligned with the canvas node cards.
contribution: Provides a visually organized, kind-coded palette UI for selecting node types from the registry and dispatching addNode requests, using shared kit components and preserving the existing interaction contract.
summary_for_embedding: Palette.tsx is a React component that renders a node-type palette from an object_info registry, grouping entries by kind into tone-coded lanes of actionable kit Surface cards and dispatching addNode on selection.
intention_for_embedding: To redesign the palette region into a recognition-by-sight rack using shared kit components and design-system kind tones while preserving the existing add-node behavior.

symbols.does: Exported functional React component that renders the grouped node-type palette and wires card clicks to addNode.
symbols.description: Palette reads node types from AppContext's oinfo registry, groups them by kind into ordered lanes, and renders each type as a tone-coded Surface card. It keeps the original data-ui-ref and addNode interaction while presenting the palette as a scannable, shared-kit rack.

symbols.does: Local comparator helper that assigns sort priority to kinds so process and content appear before other kinds.
symbols.description: order is a small inline function used inside the kinds sort. It returns 0 for 'process', 1 for 'content', and 2 for anything else, ensuring the live kinds are listed first and remaining kinds fall back to alphabetical order.
